questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
With reference to Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF), consider the following statements:
1. It can be blended up to 50% in conventional aviation turbine fuel.
2. India has mandated a 1% SAF blending in jet fuel for all domestic and international flights starting from 2027.
3. Wastewater treatment sludge can be utilized as a feedstock for its production.

How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: SAF can be blended up to 50% in conventional aviation turbine fuel (ATF). Statement 2 is incorrect: India has mandated a 1% SAF blending specifically in jet fuel sold to international airlines from 2027, not domestic flights. Statement 3 is correct: Raw materials for SAF include non-petroleum feedstocks such as corn grain, oil seeds, algae, agricultural residues, forestry residues, and wastewater treatment sludge.
questions.takeaway: SAF blending mandates in India currently target international flights starting in 2027. It is a versatile fuel that utilizes diverse non-petroleum feedstocks, including waste sludge, and can be blended up to 50% with conventional jet fuel.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Can be blended up to 50% in conventional aviation turbine fuel (ATF or jet fuel). India mandated 1% SAF blending in jet fuel sold to international airlines from 2027. Raw material: Corn grain, Oil seeds, Algae, Agricultural residues, Forestry residues, Wastewater treatment sludge etc.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
Consider the following materials:
1. Algae
2. Wastewater treatment sludge
3. Forestry residues
4. Corn grain

How many of the above can be used as raw materials for the production of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF)?
questions.explanation: Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) is an alternative fuel made entirely from non-petroleum feedstocks. Valid raw materials include corn grain, oil seeds, algae, agricultural residues, forestry residues, and wastewater treatment sludge.
questions.takeaway: SAF production relies on a wide variety of biological and waste-based feedstocks rather than fossil fuels, allowing for significant reductions in aviation emissions.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: An alternative fuel made from non-petroleum feedstocks... Raw material: Corn grain, Oil seeds, Algae, Agricultural residues, Forestry residues, Wastewater treatment sludge etc.
